rationale: The citing paper discusses Kivetz, Netzer, and Srinivasan (2004) in the main argument, not just in a background list. Simonson identifies it as the original Economist subscription asymmetric-dominance demonstration and uses it as concrete prior evidence when rebutting FLB's nonreplication claims. He argues that failing to replicate it later does not negate the earlier effect, given changed subscription conditions and reported later classroom replications. This gives the citation a meaningful evidentiary role in framing the commentary's position on the reality of the AD effect.
relation_description: Simonson uses Kivetz et al. (2004) as prior empirical support for the existence of asymmetric dominance in realistic choice settings, and as a benchmark case in evaluating replication claims. The cited study functions as part of the empirical foundation for his rebuttal to claims that the effect is not real.
